stem: Evaluate the scalar line integral \( \displaystyle\int_C f\,ds \), where \( f(x,y) = ([[a]])x + ([[b]])y \) and \( C \) is the segment \( \mathbf{r}(t) = ([[p]]t,\; [[q]]t) \) for \( 0 \le t \le 2 \).
distractor_rationales: This uses \( ds = dt \), forgetting the arc-length speed factor \( |\mathbf{r}'(t)| \).
This integrates over \( 0 \le t \le 1 \) instead of \( 0 \le t \le 2 \), halving the result.
This uses \( |\mathbf{r}'(t)|^{2} \) instead of \( |\mathbf{r}'(t)| \) for the speed.
This has the wrong overall sign.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: Evaluate the scalar line integral \( \displaystyle\int_C f\,ds \), where \( f(x,y,z) = ([[c]])z \) and \( C \) is the helix \( \mathbf{r}(t) = ([[p]]\cos t,\; [[p]]\sin t,\; [[q]]t) \) for \( 0 \le t \le [[T]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets the constant speed factor \( \sqrt{p^{2}+q^{2}} \) entirely, using \( ds = dt \).
This divides by the helix radius \( p \) instead of leaving the speed factor as \( \sqrt{p^2+q^2} \).
This has the wrong overall sign.
This multiplies by an extra spurious factor of \( p \).
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by two from the correct value.

stem: Evaluate the scalar line integral \( \displaystyle\int_C f\,ds \), where \( f(x,y) = ([[a]])x + ([[b]])y \) and \( C \) is the quarter-circle \( \mathbf{r}(t) = ([[R]]\cos t,\; [[R]]\sin t) \) for \( 0 \le t \le \pi/2 \).
solution: Here \( \mathbf{r}'(t) = (-[[R]]\sin t, [[R]]\cos t) \), so \( ds = |\mathbf{r}'(t)|\,dt = [[R]]\,dt \) (constant speed on a circle). Also \( f(\mathbf{r}(t)) = ([[a]])[[R]]\cos t + ([[b]])[[R]]\sin t \). Thus \( \int_0^{\pi/2} \big(([[a]])[[R]]\cos t+([[b]])[[R]]\sin t\big)[[R]]\,dt = ([[a]])([[R]])^{2}\big[\sin t\big]_0^{\pi/2} + ([[b]])([[R]])^{2}\big[-\cos t\big]_0^{\pi/2} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets the speed factor \( R \) entirely, using \( ds=dt \) and integrating only the raw trig antiderivatives.
This has the wrong overall sign.
This double counts, as if integrating over the full quarter TWICE.
This subtracts instead of adds the two trig contributions, and drops a factor of R.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by two from the correct value.
